title: vitis coignetiae
intro: Vitis coignetiae, also known as Korean grapevine or purple leafed grapevine, is a species of grapevine native to East Asia, characterized by its deep purple leaves and clusters of small, edible grapes. The medicinal properties of Vitis coignetiae lie in its rich antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and exert anti-cancer properties, making it a valuable remedy in traditional Chinese medicine. Herbal preparations of Vitis coignetiae are commonly made into teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
